behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at watcher-to-OS event boundary (recursive directory events), not just local parsing logic. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: in-process fakes can emit idealized child-path events and miss real watcher/backend limitations. Rejection-gate: rejected Integration because behavior text requires real placement in a subdirectory of the watched root while daemon is running, which depends on actual filesystem notification behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Start the daemon against a real temp directory tree, create a nested subdirectory, write a file into it, and assert the transformed output appears in the output directory without restarting the daemon.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug is most likely in handoff between request intake, dedupe store/window config, and enqueue decision, not OS behavior. REAL-INFRA: no real network/OS primitive is required to reproduce this failure. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required, (b) no AI output dependency, (c) no multi-agentic chain. Rejection-gate: rejected Unit because behavior text is about a second processing request being discarded within a configurable window, implying cross-module state/time-window wiring rather than a single pure function.
behaviors.test_description: Wire intake + dedupe + queue modules in-process with a controllable clock, submit two requests for the same path within the configured window, and assert only one enqueue/processing action occurs; then advance beyond window and assert a new request is accepted.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly references OS emitting multiple events. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: mocked emitters can reproduce duplicates synthetically but may miss real backend burst patterns/coalescing differences that cause duplicate processing. Rejection-gate: rejected Integration because the concrete failure source is real OS filesystem event behavior for one write.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon on a real filesystem, perform a write pattern known to trigger multiple events (e.g., write+rename/flush), capture processing invocations, and assert exactly one completed processing/output artifact for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS/REAL-INFRA: directory deletion/recreation and watch reattachment are OS-level watcher lifecycle behaviors. In-process fakes usually hide handle invalidation/rebind races. Rejection-gate: rejected Integration because behavior text requires remove+recreate while daemon is running and successful resumed watching, which depends on real watcher/OS semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon on a real watched directory, delete the directory, recreate it at the same path, then add a new file and verify it is processed without restarting the daemon process.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary includes filesystem arrival timing and timestamp metadata behavior under concurrent writes. MOCK-HIDE: synthetic in-process events can enforce perfect ordering and hide real timestamp resolution/tie and watcher scheduling issues. Rejection-gate: rejected Integration because behavior text ties ordering to creation timestamps for simultaneously arriving real files.
behaviors.test_description: Create multiple files near-simultaneously in the watched directory on a real filesystem with controlled timestamp differences (or known ties), observe actual processing order, and assert it matches required creation-time ordering policy.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: partial-write visibility and event timing are OS/filesystem dependent. MOCK-HIDE: fake writers often present atomic completion and miss race conditions between event fire and file-close. Rejection-gate: rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly requires handling a file still being written when arrival event fires, which is a real filesystem timing condition.
behaviors.test_description: With daemon running, begin a slow/chunked write to a file in the watched directory (keeping the writer open), verify processing does not start/complete early, then close writer and assert processing starts afterward and uses full file contents.
